questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: quality / compromising / without / costs / reduce / should
options: Costs should reduce without compromising quality
Quality compromising without reduce costs should
Compromising quality without costs should reduce
Should reduce costs without compromising quality
questions.solution: Costs should reduce without compromising quality

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: education / the / to / key / is / success
options: Education is the key to success
To success the key is education
Success is the key to education
questions.solution: Education is the key to success

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: country / infrastructure / of / development / backbone / the / is
options: The backbone of country development is infrastructure
Development of country backbone is infrastructure
Country development backbone is the infrastructure
Infrastructure is the backbone of country development
questions.solution: Infrastructure is the backbone of country development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: solve / team / the / to / problem / together / worked
options: The team worked together to solve the problem
Worked together the team to solve the problem
Together the team worked to solve the problem
To solve the problem the team together worked
questions.solution: The team worked together to solve the problem

questions.solution: Government has implemented stringent safety measures

questions.solution: Automation leads to loss of jobs

Mining practices must promote sustainable development
Must promote sustainable development mining practices

questions.solution: Mining practices must promote sustainable development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English

Improved efficiency has significantly modern technology

questions.solution: Modern technology has improved efficiency significantly

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Generator action:
options: EMF induced in conductor moving in magnetic field E = Blv
questions.solution: E = Blv sinθ. Fleming's right-hand rule.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which property does an ideal operational amplifier NOT have?
questions.solution: An ideal op-amp has infinite (not zero) open-loop gain, infinite input impedance and zero output impedance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1000 W and W₂ = 400 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1000 + 400 = 1400 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interpoles:
options: Between main poles to improve commutation
questions.solution: Interpoles produce reverse voltage to cancel reactance voltage in commutating coil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Luminous flux:
questions.solution: Luminous flux = Φ (lm). Luminous intensity I = Φ/Ω (cd).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Active power control:
questions.solution: P ∝ sinδ (power angle). Q controlled by excitation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding a pole to the open-loop transfer function generally:
options: Reduces stability and makes the response more oscillatory
questions.solution: An extra pole adds phase lag, pushing the root locus toward the right half-plane and reducing stability margins.

questions.stem: The superposition theorem is applicable only to:
questions.solution: Superposition holds for linear, bilateral networks; responses of independent sources add.

A voltage source in series with a resistance
